deliverable: 1b — VRAM ladder + large-grid stability (v0.15 ship gate)
tiling: GPUWRF_MP_COLUMN_TILING=1 (MP column tiling ON, via the 1a fix that makes it engage on the real 3D layout)
verdict: FAIL — MP column tiling alone does NOT unlock the large 1km grids
root_cause: With MP tiling capping the Thompson working set, the SMALLEST ladder rung (264x636 = 167,904 cols, the Switzerland-scale target) STILL OOMs on a SINGLE 18.72 GiB (20,098,507,880-byte) NON-Thompson allocation. At 167,904 cols the resident operational state alone is ~26 GiB (MaxInUse 26.22 GiB before the failing alloc), leaving ~6 GiB free against an 18.72 GiB single transient — physically impossible on the 32 GiB RTX 5090. The 18.72 GiB op is a full-ncol spectral/g-point or dycore transient (~14,960 elems/col = nz*~340), NOT Thompson — so MP tiling, which only caps Thompson, cannot help.
pre_tiling_reference: proofs/perf/v015/km_bench/largest_probe.json — pre-tiling OOM was a ~28 GiB Thompson temp arena at 209,880; capping THAT just exposes the next 18.72 GiB ceiling elsewhere.

ship_target_status.largest_proven_stable: 125,928 cols (~20-21 GiB, finite) -- a real but PARTIAL win vs pre-tiling

ladder_records.error: JaxRuntimeError: RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED: Out of memory while trying to allocate 109.35MiB.

recommendation: The v0.15 large-grid / 1km headline CANNOT ship as a default capability: MP column tiling is necessary-but-insufficient. The binding ceiling is a non-Thompson 18.72 GiB single allocation that needs its OWN column-tiling lane (radiation g-point / dycore) — out of scope for the v0.15 kernel-final close. Document as a SCOPE NOTE: v0.15 ships the niter=16 perf headline; large-grid 1km is a documented follow-on (next-version VRAM lane), NOT a v0.15 claim.

post_tiling_ceiling.note: With MP tiling ON, all grids <= 125,928 cols fit STABLY + FINITE (peak ~20-21 GiB, ms/step 250-510). 167,904 (Switzerland-scale) OOMs on the 18.72 GiB non-Thompson alloc. The named targets are ABOVE this ceiling.
